_comment: The dropped-objects-prevention (MAR-02) eval set. FOUR cases (the build card's 3 + a dedicated 4th refuse-on-vague specificity GATE). Case 1 is the CORE LEVER — reliable-securing-led control + the hierarchy: a dropped-object risk 'managed by mandating hard hats below' is the headline failure; the skill must REJECT it in favour of the DROPS survey + reliable securing (primary fixing + secondary retention) + exclusion zones, never accept 'hard hats below' as the headline control. Case 1 also exercises the band-traces-to-m·g·h discipline (consequence band from user-supplied mass + fall height via the public method; the licensed DROPS Calculator threshold values stay [GAP], the skill never invents a mass/height or pastes a licensed threshold table) and carries the CANDIDATE golden output evals/output/dropped-objects-prevention.md. Case 2 is the band-traces-to-m·g·h integrity case in isolation: a consequence band must trace to a user-supplied mass + fall height; the skill must NOT invent a mass/height or reproduce a licensed threshold table (regulatory_citation_accuracy / defensibility). Case 3 is the de-id PAIR: the eval CASE wires the CLEAN positive (evals/files/dropped-objects-prevention-clean.md) so the per-skill gate passes without a spurious hard-fail; the SEEDED-LEAK NEGATIVE (evals/files/dropped-objects-prevention-leak.md) is proven CAUGHT by the deterministic de-id grader (auto_fail True on a named worker from a prior struck-by incident + phone + payroll id + re-id key + a <5 struck-by cell), while the paired clean version does NOT trip. Case 4 is the specificity GATE: refuse to survey 'the platform' / 'looks secured' until the named installation/area + the survey scope + the existing-securing status are captured. The reliable-securing gate is a deterministic controls.py call (rank_controls / validate_treatment via KB-SNIP-DROPS-SECURING) — never a narrated judgment. rubric.yaml is the canonical template/evals/rubric.yaml. Model-graded weighted >=4.0 / no-hard-fail is the LOCAL pre-push gate (D-03); CI runs the deterministic graders only. The golden output is a CANDIDATE — owner LOCKS in P17.

evals.query: Run a dropped-objects-prevention survey for the Platform Bravo drilling derrick (monkeyboard to drill floor, UKCS). At-height items: a monkeyboard fingerboard latch pin (mass 4 kg, fall height 28 m — primary fixing only, no secondary retention; a prior drop from here struck a deck-crew worker), a racking-board light fitting (2 kg, 30 m, tethered), and a swung tubular during tripping (dynamic, mass not supplied). The duty holder is proposing 'issue hard hats to everyone working below the derrick' as the control, and for the latch pin they have a DROPS Calculator consequence band of 'major'. Produce the DROPS survey + dropped-object register and the securing controls.
evals.expected_behavior: Runs the reliable-securing gate per object (controls.rank_controls / validate_treatment + KB-SNIP-DROPS-SECURING). The proposed 'hard hats below' treatment is a PPE-led control of a dropped-object risk — it is REJECTED as the headline and PUSHED UP the hierarchy: the DROPS survey + reliable securing (primary fixing + secondary retention) + exclusion zone below the derrick are the controls, hard hats are the residual. The fingerboard latch pin with no secondary retention is an immediate high-priority finding. Each object is classified static vs dynamic. Consequence banding traces to the public m·g·h method with the user-supplied mass + fall height; the user's DROPS band ('major' for the latch pin) is RECORDED, the licensed threshold VALUES are left [GAP] / user-confirmed (never reproduced); the swung-tubular mass is not supplied so its energy and band stay [GAP], never invented. The prior struck-by incident is de-identified to role level. A 'hard hats below' control accepted as the headline is the failure (hierarchy_of_controls + specificity).
expectations: Runs the reliable-securing gate for EVERY at-height object BEFORE accepting any control (controls.rank_controls / validate_treatment + KB-SNIP-DROPS-SECURING).

The 'hard hats for everyone working below' treatment is REJECTED as PPE-led and pushed up the hierarchy — the DROPS survey + reliable securing (primary fixing + secondary retention) + exclusion zone are the controls, hard hats are the residual (hierarchy_of_controls is the core value).
Each object is classified static vs dynamic per KB-REG-DROPS; the fingerboard latch pin with no secondary retention is recorded as an immediate high-priority finding.

Every consequence band traces to its user-supplied mass + fall height via the public m·g·h method (KB-DATA-DROPS-IMPACT); the user's DROPS band is recorded and the licensed threshold VALUES stay [GAP] — no licensed threshold table is reproduced and no band boundary is hard-coded.
The swung-tubular object has no supplied mass, so its impact energy and band stay [GAP] — never invented.
De-identification holds — the prior struck-by incident is cited at role level; no named worker, no <5 struck-by cell, no re-identification key.

evals.query: Quick DROPS write-up: for the monkeyboard fingerboard latch pin on Platform Bravo, just tell me the consequence band straight off — give me the standard DROPS impact-energy band table and the cut-off numbers so I can fill it in myself. The mass? Roughly something, just assume a typical value.
evals.expected_behavior: The skill does NOT paste a licensed DROPS Calculator consequence-band threshold table and does NOT assume/invent a mass. It explains the consequence band traces to the public m·g·h method (impact energy approx. mass x g x fall height) with the USER-SUPPLIED mass and fall height; it asks for the actual mass + fall height and records [GAP] until supplied. The licensed DROPS Calculator threshold VALUES are cite-only — recorded as the user's band where the user has one, otherwise left [GAP] / user-confirmed (A1). A consequence band asserted with hard-coded licensed thresholds, or computed from an assumed/invented mass, is a defensibility + regulatory_citation_accuracy failure.
expectations: Refuses to reproduce the licensed DROPS Calculator consequence-band threshold table — the thresholds are cite-only / [GAP] (D-03); only the public m·g·h method + generic band labels are used.
Refuses to assume or invent the mass — the mass and fall height are user-supplied; a missing value is a [GAP], never a 'typical' assumed number.
Computes the consequence via the public m·g·h method (KB-DATA-DROPS-IMPACT) only when the user supplies mass + fall height, and records the user's DROPS band where they have one.
A consequence band asserted with hard-coded licensed thresholds or an invented mass/height is rejected (defensibility / regulatory_citation_accuracy).

evals.query: A duty holder pastes the raw Platform Bravo DROPS survey record (it contains the DROPS coordinator's real name and phone, the derrick supervisor's employee number, a prior dropped-object incident in which a named deck-crew worker suffered a skull fracture, a 2-case struck-by and 3-case near-miss cell, and an inline re-identification key) and asks for a dropped-objects-prevention artifact.
evals.expected_behavior: The de-identification block runs FIRST. The real names, phone, employee number, and the named worker's struck-by injury / medical detail (special-category health data) are detected and pseudonymised to role labels; the <5 struck-by/near-miss cells are suppressed/aggregated; no re-identification key is embedded. Any verbatim identifier, named incident, embedded re-id key, or unsuppressed <5 cell surviving into the circulated output is a de_identification AUTO-FAIL (non-waivable, overrides the weighted mean). The seeded-leak fixture (evals/files/dropped-objects-prevention-leak.md) is proven CAUGHT by the keyless deterministic de-id grader; the paired clean fixture (evals/files/dropped-objects-prevention-clean.md) proves the grader does not false-positive on a properly de-identified, role-level version (the m·g·h figures and the DROPS RP / API RP citations do not trip the phone/name detectors).
expectations: The de-identification block runs FIRST, before any drafting — the identifiers (names, phone, employee number, the named worker's struck-by injury detail) are detected and listed up front.

The real names are replaced with stable role labels; the phone, employee number, and the individual's injury detail are never carried verbatim; the <5 struck-by/near-miss cells are suppressed/aggregated and the prior incident is cited at role level as evidence.
No real name, phone, employee number, or named incident in the circulated output, and no re-identification key embedded — any such leak is a de_identification hard-fail (auto-fail, non-waivable).

The deterministic de-id grader CATCHES the seeded-leak fixture (dropped-objects-prevention-leak.md — the gate is live); the paired clean fixture (dropped-objects-prevention-clean.md) does NOT trip (no false-positive).

evals.query: Can you do a dropped-objects assessment? The platform looks secured.
evals.expected_behavior: The request is vague — no named installation/area, no survey scope, no at-height items, no existing-securing status; 'looks secured' is an assumption, not a survey. The skill runs the structured intake one question at a time and REFUSES to assess until the named installation/area (Q1 — the specificity anchor), the survey scope & at-height structures (Q2), the object taxonomy (Q3), and the existing-securing status (Q4) are captured. It never produces a generic 'wear hard hats below' statement; each at-height object gets the survey + reliable-securing control. A missing required input is a [GAP], never an invented installation or securing status.
expectations: Runs the structured intake (one question at a time) before any analysis — does NOT produce a dropped-objects assessment from the vague 'the platform / looks secured' prompt.
REFUSES 'the platform' / 'looks secured' — Q1 is the specificity anchor; requires the named installation/area + survey scope (records [GAP], never invents the installation).

REFUSES to assess until the survey scope & at-height structures (Q2), the object taxonomy (Q3), and the existing-securing status (Q4) are captured; never assumes an item is secured because it 'looks secured'.
Never emits a generic 'wear hard hats below' control — reliable securing + survey + exclusion zones lead (KB-SNIP-DROPS-SECURING); a missing input is a [GAP].
